title: Stop and check risk
summary: This may require a professional. Do not start work involving electricity, gas, structure or major water issues.
steps: Secure the area only if safe.
Take a photo and contact a professional.
warnings: Do not perform electrical, gas or structural work using this app.

title: High risk — safe decision
summary: The safe plan is to secure the place and call the right professional.
steps: Do not start the repair yourself.
If there is water, close the main valve only if you know how and can access it safely.
If you smell gas, leave the place and call emergency help; do not use switches.
For electrical issues, switch off the breaker only if it is safe.
Take photos from a distance and describe symptoms.
Call a plumber, electrician, gas technician or building specialist.
dontDo: do not work around active leaks or electricity

title: Squeaky door
summary: Start by cleaning and lightly lubricating the hinge.
steps: Apply a small amount of lubricant.
warnings: Do not use too much oil.

title: Squeaky door — full guide
summary: A simple plan without removing the door.
steps: Protect the floor with paper or cloth.
Apply one drop of hinge lubricant.
If the hinge is bent or still noisy, stop and consider help.
dontDo: do not remove heavy doors alone

title: Loose hinge
summary: Check loose screws and material damage first.
steps: Check whether the hinge moves in the material.

title: Loose hinge — full guide
summary: Plan for cabinet, light door or front hinge.
steps: If the screw spins, the hole is worn.
For light furniture use a longer screw or repair plug matched to material.
Stop if the door is heavy, dropping or cracked.
dontDo: do not use full power driver
do not repair heavy entrance doors alone

title: Small furniture scratch
summary: Clean the surface and check whether the scratch is shallow.
steps: Use repair wax or marker in similar color.
warnings: Test product in a hidden place first.

title: Furniture scratch — full guide
summary: Safe masking of small scratches without full renovation.
steps: Choose marker, crayon or wax color.
Apply small amounts with the grain.
For antiques, lacquered surfaces or deep scratches, consider a professional.
dontDo: do not sand without a plan

title: Basic clogged drain
summary: Start with simple methods without harsh chemicals.
steps: Use a plunger if water drains slowly.
warnings: For backup, flooding or several clogged drains, call a plumber.

title: Clogged drain — full guide
summary: Safe plan for a light sink or shower clog.
steps: Use a plunger in short motions.
Clean an accessible trap only if you can reassemble it.
Stop if water backs up, leaks or smells like sewage.
dontDo: do not dismantle plumbing without knowledge

title: Replace silicone
summary: Remove loose old silicone and dry the place first.
steps: Do not apply new silicone to a wet place.
warnings: Mold, hidden leaks or moving cabin require broader inspection.

title: Silicone — full guide
summary: Small bathroom or kitchen silicone replacement plan.
steps: Remove residues with a plastic scraper.
Apply a thin even line of sanitary silicone.
dontDo: do not cover mold with new silicone

title: Refresh grout
summary: Clean and check whether grout is dirty or damaged.
steps: Use a grout renovator only on dry surface.
warnings: Cracking or falling grout may mean a bigger problem.

title: Grout — refresh guide
summary: Plan for dirty but stable grout between tiles.
steps: Clean with brush and mild cleaner.
Apply grout marker or renovator as instructed.
For large gaps consider regrouting or professional help.
dontDo: do not use harsh chemicals without ventilation

title: Stuck zipper
summary: First check whether fabric is caught in the slider.
steps: Move slider back gently and clean track.
warnings: Pulling can permanently damage the zipper.

title: Zipper — full guide
summary: Safe steps for a stuck zipper in clothes or bag.
steps: Move slider back millimeter by millimeter.
Use a little pencil graphite or zipper product.
If teeth are missing or zipper splits, replace it or call a tailor.
dontDo: do not use oil on delicate fabric

title: Small sealing
summary: Find the source and confirm this is a small surface issue.
steps: Do not seal an active leak without diagnosis.
warnings: For water, gas, electricity or structure, stop and call a professional.

title: Small seal — full guide
summary: Plan for a small gap with no active leak.
steps: Choose silicone, sealing tape or repair compound for the surface.
Apply a thin layer and smooth it.
Watch after drying; if it returns, call a professional.

title: Safe first step
summary: Start with risk, tools and scope assessment.
steps: Check that it is not electricity, gas, structure or major water.
Prepare simple tools and work slowly.
warnings: If you do not understand the part, do not dismantle it.

title: Universal small repair plan
summary: Method for simple low-risk home repairs.
steps: Prepare light, gloves and basic tools.
If the problem returns, grows or touches installations, call a professional.
dontDo: do not work without securing the area
